data.title: Semler Scientific plans further Bitcoin investments and targets QuantaFlo product expansion in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Semler Scientific, Inc. (NASDAQ:SMLR) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Doug Murphy-Chutorian highlighted strong performance and cash generation from healthcare operations in 2024. He emphasized the company's focus on its Bitcoin treasury strategy, with Bitcoin now serving as its primary treasury reserve asset.
  • Chairman Eric Semler noted that Semler Scientific owns 3,192 Bitcoins valued at over $300 million, making the company the tenth-largest corporate Bitcoin holder. He also stated that the company has generated a Bitcoin yield of 152% and sees Bitcoin as a highly accretive long-term investment.
  • COO Jennifer Oliva Herrington emphasized the company's commitment to expanding its core product, QuantaFlo, which aids in diagnosing peripheral arterial disease (PAD). She announced plans to seek a new 510(k) clearance for a product extension of QuantaFlo in 2025 and explore additional cardiovascular applications.
  • CFO Renae Cormier reported total Q4 revenues of $12.4 million, a decrease of 18% year-over-year, but highlighted an increase in income from operations to $3.5 million, up 40% from the prior year. She noted a $28.8 million gain in fair value from Bitcoin holdings and detailed the company’s adoption of a new accounting standard for crypto assets.

Outlook

  • Management disclosed plans to continue growing its Bitcoin holdings using operating cash, proceeds from equity offerings, and other financing strategies. Eric Semler mentioned the potential for further equity or financing options to maximize stakeholder value.
  • COO Herrington outlined goals for 2025, including mitigating revenue declines in QuantaFlo while maintaining infrastructure to support innovation and growth in cardiovascular care solutions. New products in the cardiovascular space are being test-marketed, with formal introductions contingent on material revenue generation.
  • The company is targeting additional market penetration by expanding its customer base beyond managed care into clinics and value-based care providers, aiming to solidify QuantaFlo as the standard of care.

Financial Results

  • Total Q4 2024 revenues were $12.4 million, down 18% year-over-year, attributed to CMS rate adjustments. Operating expenses decreased 29% to $8.9 million, contributing to a 40% increase in income from operations to $3.5 million.
  • Net income for the quarter was $29.2 million or $3.64 per basic share, bolstered by a $28.8 million gain on Bitcoin holdings. At year-end, the company held $8.9 million in cash and equivalents.
  • The company reported purchasing 1,280 Bitcoins during Q4 for $121.3 million. Subsequent to the quarter end, an additional 894 Bitcoins were acquired for $90.7 million, bringing total Bitcoin holdings to 3,192 with an average cost basis of $87,854 per Bitcoin.

Q&A

  • Analyst (via email): Why did you proceed with a convertible note offering? Eric Semler: The offering was pursued as a cost-effective, less dilutive funding option. Strong demand allowed the size to increase to $100 million, and the capped call created a 75% conversion premium to the stock price at issuance.
  • Analyst (via email): What do you expect for 2025? Jennifer Oliva Herrington: The company aims to sustain QuantaFlo revenues despite CMS rate pressures by expanding its customer base and maintaining infrastructure to support growth in cardiovascular care solutions.
  • Analyst (via email): What are your expectations from the new government regarding crypto? Eric Semler: There is optimism around the repeal of SAB 121, which could allow major banks to custody Bitcoin, potentially encouraging other public companies to add Bitcoin to their balance sheets.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about revenue pressures and sought clarity on the company's Bitcoin-focused strategy and future growth initiatives. Their tone was neutral but cautious, asking for further details on 2025 expectations.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, especially about Bitcoin's long-term potential and its integration with the company's financial strategy. Phrases like "we have strong conviction" and "we remain committed" underscored their optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained steady, though the Q&A included more detailed justifications for the Bitcoin strategy, reflecting heightened analyst scrutiny.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenues declined from $13.5 million in Q3 to $12.4 million in Q4, with management attributing this to CMS rate adjustments.
  • Bitcoin holdings increased significantly from 1,058 in Q3 to 3,192 in Q4, reflecting aggressive investment through operating cash and equity proceeds.
  • Analyst queries in Q4 focused more on the sustainability of the Bitcoin strategy and its impact on shareholder value, whereas Q3 questions were more operationally focused.
  • Management's tone remained confident in both quarters, but Q4 involved more emphasis on justifying the Bitcoin treasury strategy and its accretive potential.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential volatility in reported net income due to the adoption of a new crypto accounting standard but emphasized that it does not affect cash flow.
  • Revenue pressures from CMS rate adjustments were highlighted as an ongoing challenge for 2025.
  • Analyst concerns revolved around the sustainability and potential dilution risks associated with the company’s Bitcoin-heavy strategy.

Final Takeaway

Semler Scientific highlighted its dual focus on expanding its QuantaFlo product line and aggressively growing its Bitcoin holdings. While revenues faced pressure due to CMS rate adjustments, management underscored its confidence in Bitcoin's long-term value and its strategic potential to enhance shareholder returns. Looking ahead, the company aims to mitigate revenue declines, advance cardiovascular product offerings, and explore accretive financing options to further its Bitcoin investments.

data.title: Great Lakes Dredge & Dock outlines $1.2B backlog driving strong 2025 revenue visibility
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Great Lakes Dredge & Dock (NASDAQ:GLDD) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Lasse Petterson highlighted that Great Lakes achieved its second-highest financial performance in 135 years, with Q4 revenues of $202.8 million and adjusted EBITDA of $40.2 million. The full-year revenue reached $762.7 million with adjusted EBITDA at $136 million.
  • The company secured a $1.2 billion backlog, with 94% focused on capital projects such as port deepening and coastal protection, which are expected to provide high asset utilization in 2025 and revenue visibility into 2026.
  • The new hopper dredge, Galveston Island, contributed significantly to 2024 results, and its sister ship, Amelia Island, is expected to begin operations in the second half of 2025.
  • Petterson noted that a new business initiative called Offshore Energy will focus on international markets, including offshore wind and subsea infrastructure protection.
  • CFO Scott Kornblau stated, "Revenues of $202.8 million in the fourth quarter increased $21.1 million from the prior year's fourth quarter, primarily due to the addition of the Galveston Island and higher capital and coastal protection revenue."

Outlook

  • Management expects $720 million of the $1.2 billion backlog to convert into revenue during 2025, with additional "book and burn" opportunities to further increase revenue.
  • The company anticipates capital expenditures for 2025 to be between $140 million and $160 million, mainly for completing the Acadia and Amelia Island vessels.
  • Petterson mentioned that the Army Corps of Engineers' budget for 2025 is expected to reach a record $10 billion, which could sustain a strong bid market.
  • The company broadened its offshore energy focus to include international markets, with Acadia expected to target opportunities in Europe and Asia starting in 2026.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenues of $202.8 million increased year-over-year by $21.1 million, driven by higher capital and coastal protection revenue.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2024 was $40.2 million, with a margin of 20%.
  • Gross profit increased to $48.9 million, with margins improving to 24.1% due to better project execution and a focus on higher-margin projects.
  • Full-year 2024 results included a net income of $57.3 million, more than four times the prior year's figure.
  • The company ended 2024 with a liquidity position of over $300 million, driven by operational cash flow and a $150 million second lien term loan.

Q&A

  • Joe Gomes, NOBLE Capital: Asked about potential market impacts from defense spending reviews. CEO Lasse Petterson responded, "We are very certain on our backlog execution for 2025," with minimal expected impact from delays in bid markets.
  • Jon Tanwanteng, CJS Securities: Asked about Acadia's construction progress. Petterson noted the vessel is on track for delivery by late 2025 or early 2026, with international opportunities already under consideration.
  • Adam Thalhimer, Thompson Davis: Queried revenue guidance and margins for 2025. CFO Kornblau stated, "Even with the seven dry docks, my expectation is we will see revenue higher in 2025 than we did in 2024," while maintaining strong margins due to high-value project mix.
  • Julio Romero, Sidoti & Company: Inquired about international opportunities for Acadia. Petterson highlighted increasing demand for subsea infrastructure protection in Europe and Asia, driven by geopolitical concerns.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, with questions focused on backlog liquidation, offshore energy opportunities, and margin sustainability. Gomes and Romero emphasized the importance of Acadia's utilization and the international market outlook.
  • Management projected confidence during prepared remarks, often using phrases like "we are very certain" and "we are well positioned." However, responses in the Q&A section reflected a cautious tone regarding potential market delays and external risks.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management displayed increased confidence due to the significant backlog and improved financial performance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Backlog remained steady at $1.2 billion, but 2024 saw a shift in focus towards international markets for the Offshore Energy segment.
  • Revenue increased from $191.2 million in Q3 to $202.8 million in Q4, with margins also improving due to better project execution.
  • Analysts in the current quarter concentrated more on international opportunities and external risks, whereas previous quarter questions were centered around U.S. offshore wind and LNG projects.
  • Management's tone was more confident this quarter, backed by strong financial results and substantial backlog visibility into 2025 and 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Potential delays in federal funding approvals and bid market timing could affect 2025 project awards.
  • The Jones Act ruling on rock installation could impact offshore wind projects, though management stated no immediate changes to Acadia's position.
  • International market entry for Acadia involves risks related to contract timing and geopolitical factors.
  • Dry docking costs for seven vessels in 2025 will weigh on margins.

Final Takeaway

Great Lakes Dredge & Dock closed 2024 with strong financial performance and a robust $1.2 billion backlog, providing visibility into 2025 and beyond. Management emphasized the successful execution of capital and coastal protection projects and highlighted international opportunities for its Offshore Energy segment. Though facing potential risks from federal funding delays and dry dock costs, the company remains well positioned for growth in both the domestic and international markets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Skeena Resources in C$70.5M bought deal financing for Eskay Creek project
data.description:
Skeena Resources (NYSE:SKE) -5.2% post-market Tuesday after saying a syndicate of underwriters led by BMO Capital Markets agreed to purchase 4.8M common shares of the company at C$14.70/share on a bought deal basis, for total gross proceeds of ~C$70.5M, with an underwriters option to increase the offering size by 15%.

Skeena (NYSE:SKE) said the proceeds will be used for continued advancement of the Eskay Creek gold-silver project in British Columbia and for general corporate purposes.

Eskay Creek will be one of the highest-grade and lowest cost open-pit precious metals mines in the world, with substantial silver by-product production that surpasses many primary silver mines, the company said.

data.title: TPG RE Finance Trust GAAP EPS of $0.09 misses by $0.11
data.description:
• TPG RE Finance Trust press release (NYSE:TRTX): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.09 misses by $0.11.
  • Generated Distributable Earnings and Distributable Earnings Before Realized Losses of $7.8 million, or $0.10 per common share, and $17.6 million, or $0.22 per common share, respectively.
  • Carried at quarter-end an allowance for credit losses of $64.0 million, a decrease of $5.3 million from $69.3 million as of September 30, 2024.
  • Recognized credit loss expense of $4.6 million, or $0.06 per basic and diluted common share.

data.title: Sapiens outlines 2025 guidance with focus on AI, cloud growth, and $553M-$558M revenue target
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sapiens International Corporation N.V. (NASDAQ:SPNS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Sapiens reported Q4 2024 revenue of $134M, reflecting a 2.6% increase year-over-year. CEO Roni Al-Dor highlighted key achievements, including signing over 15 deals in Q4 across Life and P&C businesses, with three significant insurance platform deals completed globally since its launch in June 2024.

  • The company ended 2024 with 169 customers transitioned to Sapiens Cloud, emphasizing its focus on accelerating cloud adoption. North America delivered robust growth, with five new and existing customer wins in Q4, alongside a strong pipeline for its Life business reaching historic levels.

  • Significant product innovation was noted with the release of the P&C Core Suite version 12.1, featuring technology upgrades, operational enhancements, and integration with Sapiens Digital Suite. Additionally, the company plans to integrate AI-driven automation and analytics into its P&C platform in 2025.

  • CFO Roni Giladi highlighted that recurring and reoccurring revenue represented 72.5% of Q4 2024 total revenue, compared to 69.1% in the prior year, contributing to a gross margin improvement to 46.7%.

Outlook

  • Guidance for 2025 projects non-GAAP revenue between $553M and $558M, with an operating profit range of $98M to $102M. This guidance assumes a currency headwind of 1% to revenue growth due to euro and pound weakening.

  • Management aims to achieve 60% customer cloud adoption within five years, up from the current 28%, leveraging SaaS solutions with AI-driven capabilities. The company plans to drive cross-selling opportunities with existing customers across its platform offerings.

  • Sapiens expects a low single-digit revenue growth rate in 2025, citing continued SaaS transition impacts, macroeconomic uncertainty, and extended sales cycles in Europe.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 annualized recurring revenue (ARR) reached $175M, a 6.5% increase year-over-year. Non-recurring revenue declined due to the SaaS transition and delayed deal signings.

  • Gross profit for Q4 was $63M, with an operating profit of $24M and net income of $21M, reflecting a 3.1% increase compared to Q4 2023. EPS for the quarter was $0.37, up from $0.36 in the prior year.

  • Full-year 2024 revenue totaled $542M, a 5.4% increase compared to 2023, with recurring and reoccurring revenue contributing $390M, representing 72% of total revenue.

Q&A

  • Dylan Becker, William Blair: Asked about cloud migration and cross-selling initiatives. President Roni Al-Dor explained the company is confident in accelerating cloud adoption and focusing on modular platform capabilities to drive deeper customer engagements.

  • Chris Reimer, Barclays: Inquired about M&A plans. CEO Al-Dor stated the company is actively pursuing opportunities, aiming to complete at least one acquisition in 2025, with a focus on small to mid-sized firms in North America and Europe.

  • Alexei Gogolev, JPMorgan: Questioned customer concentration trends and cloud transition goals. CFO Giladi confirmed customer concentration remains low, with no single customer representing more than 5% of revenue. He also outlined plans to migrate 15-30 existing customers annually to the cloud.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on SaaS transition impacts and macroeconomic challenges. Questions centered on revenue growth drivers, competitive dynamics, and strategic investments.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, reiterating long-term growth potential through AI integration, cloud adoption, and cross-selling. CFO Giladi highlighted improved margins and recurring revenue growth as key strengths.

  • Compared to the prior quarter, management’s tone reflected stronger confidence in its AI and cloud strategies, while analysts remained consistent in their cautious outlook.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue declined sequentially from $137M in Q3 to $134M in Q4, attributed to SaaS transition and currency headwinds. ARR growth remained robust, increasing from $173M in Q3 to $175M in Q4.

  • Management’s strategic focus shifted towards accelerating cloud migration and increasing cross-selling, while analysts continued to probe SaaS impacts and competitive pressures.

  • Sentiment among analysts remained cautious, consistent with Q3, while management showed increased confidence in its AI-driven platform innovation and strategic partnerships.

Risks and Concerns

  • Key risks identified include extended sales cycles in Europe, competitive pressures in the North American P&C market, and macroeconomic uncertainties affecting deal closures.

  • Management plans to mitigate risks through product innovation, expanding system integrator partnerships, and leveraging AI-driven solutions to enhance operational efficiency.

Final Takeaway

Sapiens International Corporation reported modest revenue growth for Q4 2024, with recurring revenues driving improved margins. The company remains focused on accelerating cloud adoption, cross-selling opportunities, and integrating advanced AI capabilities across its platform. Guidance for 2025 reflects cautious optimism, balancing growth potential against external challenges. Strategic investments in innovation and partnerships position Sapiens for long-term sustainable growth, despite near-term headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gibson Energy reports FY results

data.title: Trump says 25% tariffs targeting cars, chips & pharma imports coming as soon as April 2
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would probably announce 25% tariffs on imports of cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals on April 2, marking a widening of targeted sectors after previously imposing levies on imports of steel and aluminum.

Trump spoke to reporters at his Mar-a-Lago residence. "I probably will tell you that on April 2, but it will be in the neighborhood of 25%," he said in response to a question on what the automobile tariff rate will be.

When asked a follow-up question on the rate for semiconductors and pharmaceuticals, he said: "It'll be 25% and higher, and it'll go very substantially higher over the course of the year, but we want to give them time to come in because, as you know, when they come in to the United States, and they have their plant or factory here, there is no tariff. So we want to give them a little bit of a chance."

Aside from the earlier announced tariffs on steel and aluminum imports, Trump has also tasked his administration with investigating current trade relations with the U.S. and proposing reciprocal tariffs on a country-specific basis.

Before taking the questions at Mar-a-Lago, Trump said that he had been "contacted by some of the biggest companies in the world" about coming back and setting up operations in the U.S. He did not take any specific names.

"Because of what we're doing economically and through tariffs and taxes and incentives, and they want to come back into the United States. And we'll be announcing various very large companies, the biggest, actually. And they'll be coming back having to do with chips and having to do also with cars and lots of other things," the President said.

"We're going to be announcing some very, very big, very big, momentous, I think momentous decisions are being made by companies all around the world, the biggest, and they want to come back into the U.S. Car plants are being canceled in other locations now because they want to build them here," he added.

Tariffs have dominated headlines since Trump took office. The President has also targeted Canada and Mexico with 25% tariffs and China with 10%. The two North American nations showed a willingness to work with Trump, leading to a delay in the implementation of the levies. The Asian country, on the other hand, responded with its own set of tariffs.

Wall Street (SP500) has largely remained resilient to the tariff drama, hovering at record highs. Still, Bank of America's latest fund manager survey showed a big jump in respondents who now see global trade war as the most bearish development for risk assets in 2025.

Here are some popular exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

Here are some China-linked ETFs: (KWEB), (PGJ), (CQQQ), (FXI), (GXC), (MCHI), (FLCH), (CNYA), (ASHR), and (YINN).

Some Canada-linked ETFs: (EWC), (FXC), (FLCA), and (BBCA).

And some Mexico-linked ones: (EWW), (FLMX), and (MEXX).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Celanese’s stock drops on quarterly loss, weak demand
data.description:
Celanese’s (NYSE:CE) stock fell 13% in extended trading Tuesday after the maker of specialty materials and chemicals reported a quarterly loss. The company is grappling with weak demand from the automotive, paints and industrial sectors that is expected to last into 2025.

For the three-month period ended in December, Celanese (NYSE:CE) swung to a loss of $1.91 billion from a profit of $701 million a year earlier.

Revenue slumped 8% to $2.37 billion, in line with the average estimate among analysts surveyed by S&P Global Intelligence. Celanese (CE) blamed the drop on declines product prices and sales volume, along with foreign currency moves.

The automotive and industrial sectors cut inventory in engineered materials, hurting fourth-quarter financials, according to Celanese (CE). The company lowered expenses and adjusted its output to match weaker demand, which pressured its working capital.

Weakness in demand and pricing are expected to continue into 2025, management said said. Celanese (CE) forecast earnings in the first quarter to be $0.25 to $0.50 a share amid soft demand. Second-quarter earnings are estimated at about $1 a share higher than the first quarter, according to the company.

Celanese (CE) plans to close its Luxembourg Mylar Specialty Films manufacturing site, which it co-owns with Teijin, to reduce expenses.

"With little indication of near-term recovery, it is our job to drive productivity and earnings growth at Celanese even if fundamental demand remains flat or declines further," Scott Richardson, chief executive since January, said in a statement.

data.title: Exxon's fourth FPSO facility for Guyana departs from Singapore
data.description:
The fourth floating production storage and offloading facility to be operated by the Exxon Mobil-led (NYSE:XOM) consortium in Guyana has departed from Singapore on its route to the massive offshore project, Retuters reported Tuesday.

The One Guyana FPSO vessel was built by SBM Offshore (OTCPK:SBFFF) with a 250K bbl/day production capacity; once it is installed, the consortium plans to raise production capacity to 940K bbl/day later this year, after its output averaged 616K bbl/day last year after upgrades at two of its three facilities.

The Exxon-led group (NYSE:XOM) also submitted an application for an environmental authorization to develop the Hammerhead discovery offshore Guyana, making it the seventh major project in the Stabroek block.

The consortium plans to produce up to 180K bbl/day of oil and 95M cf/day of gas at Hammerhead, according to a document published by the country's environmental agency on Monday.

The Exxon-led (XOM) group, which includes Hess (HES) and China's CNOOC (OTCPK:CEOHF), has waited for the government's approval for the project since last year; it also said last week that it requested approval for its eighth project, Longtail.
